hawkish_phrases: Domestic inflation remained high, as wages were still rising at an elevated pace
services inflation was still hovering at rates around 4%
it was too early to declare victory in terms of a timely and sustainable return to target
keep policy rates sufficiently restrictive for as long as necessary
dovish_phrases: incoming information on inflation showed that the disinflationary process was well on track
Indicators of economic activity had surprised somewhat to the downside
headline inflation had fallen further to its lowest level since April 2021
inflation was expected to decline to target in the course of 2025
the risks from cutting at the present meeting and potentially being too early were lower than the risks of waiting and potentially acting too late
summary: The ECB cut rates by 25 basis points unanimously in October 2024, citing well-tracked disinflation and weaker-than-expected economic activity, with risk management considerations supporting action now over waiting, though noting domestic inflation and wages remain elevated.
